skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Cap...
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation wrapper for inference.sh CLI that provides usage examples for Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview. The detected backticks are markdown code block delimiters, not Ruby execution. URLs are legitimate service endpoints required for the image generation functionality. No cryptographic code or malicious patterns exist in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected Ruby/shell backtick execution at multiple lines. These are markdown code block delimiters (```) used for documenting bash commands, not actual code execution. The skill is documentation-only with no executable Ruby code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation file containing bash command examples. Triple backticks are standard markdown code fence syntax, not Ruby backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URLs (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate service endpoints: inference.sh (the CLI tool being used), example.com (placeholder in demo), and documentation links. Required for skill functionality.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to inference.sh service and documentation - legitimate endpoints required for the image generation skill to function.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged content as cryptographic algorithms. Lines contain YAML frontmatter, JSON parameters, and table formatting - no cryptographic code exists.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code present. Content consists of YAML metadata, image generation parameters (num_images), and markdown table formatting.

content.value_statement: Create stunning images using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image model through inference.sh CLI. Generate text-to-image, edit photos, and produce multiple variations with Google Search grounding for real-time context.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview
Edit existing images by providing up to 14 input images
Create multiple image variations in a single request
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time contextual generation
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Image generation credits apply via inference.sh service
Maximum 14 input images for editing tasks

use_cases.title: Quick Image Creation
use_cases.description: Generate single images from text prompts for website assets, social media, or presentations

use_cases.title: Product Visualization
use_cases.description: Create multiple variations of product images for e-commerce or advertising campaigns

use_cases.title: Concept Art Generation
use_cases.description: Produce artistic visualizations with Google Search grounding for current events or trending topics

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your image] using nano-banana-2 skill
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 4 variations of [describe subject] with different styles using nano-banana-2
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need options to choose from

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the uploaded image to [describe changes] using nano-banana-2
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to modify an existing image

prompt_templates.title: High-Resolution Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 4K resolution image of [describe scene] with [style details] using nano-banana-2

output_examples.output: Returns generated image URL(s) with metadata including description and pricing info

output_examples.input: Minimalist logo for coffee shop, 4 variations
output_examples.output: Returns array of 4 logo image URLs with descriptions

best_practices: Be specific in prompts - include style, lighting, composition details for better results
Use aspect ratio matching your intended output (16:9 for landscape, 9:16 for social)
Enable Google Search grounding for current events or accurate location references
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make something cool' - be specific about subject and style
Do not exceed 14 input images for editing - will cause errors
Avoid requesting resolutions higher than 4K - not supported

faq.question: What is nano-banana-2?
faq.answer: Nano-banana-2 is a skill for generating images using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the inference.sh CLI tool.

faq.question: Do I need an account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install inference.sh CLI and create an account at inference.sh to use this skill.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide up to 14 input images for editing tasks. The model will modify them based on your prompt.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Input images can be JPEG, PNG, or WebP format. Output format is configurable.

faq.question: What is Google Search grounding?
faq.answer: When enabled, the model uses real-time Google Search to provide accurate, current information in your generated images, useful for weather, news, or location-specific content.

faq.question: What is the resolution limit?
faq.answer: Maximum resolution is 4K. You can also choose 1K or 2K resolutions.
